scope: US-admitted attorneys who have handled PA cases at appellate level, published in the field, or testified/consulted as expert witnesses. Stance is sourced, not inferred.

stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA as a legitimate, diagnosable phenomenon worth litigating
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA is misused in family court, especially against DV survivors
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both family violence and alienating-behaviour frames; AFCC-aligned differentiated approach

practitioners.name: Ashish S. Joshi, JD
practitioners.firm: Joshi, Attorneys + Counselors (successor to Lorandos Joshi PC)
named_cases: Litigating Parental Alienation: Evaluating and Presenting an Effective Case in Court (ABA Publishing, 2021)
Leave No Child Behind: Parental Alienation in Family Courts, ABA Litigation Journal Summer 2020-21
practitioners.hook: Author of the ABA's only book-length litigation manual on PA — gateway to the entire ABA recognition-camp network.
practitioners.notes: Firm rebranded after Lorandos's retirement. Named appellate citations from his casework not publicly enumerated on firm site.

practitioners.name: Demosthenes Lorandos, PhD, JD
practitioners.firm: PsychLaw (founder; retired; firm now led by Shawn A. Wygant)
named_cases: Parental Alienation: Science and Law (Charles C. Thomas, 2020) — Appendix B catalogues 1,000+ PA trial and appellate cases
Harman & Lorandos (2020), Allegations of Family Violence in Court: How Parental Alienation Affects Judicial Outcomes, Psychology, Public Policy, and Law
practitioners.hook: Architect of the appellate-case catalogue the recognition camp relies on — open via Wygant.
practitioners.notes: Now retired per firm site. Direct post-retirement contact path not publicly confirmed.

practitioners.name: Jeffery M. Leving, JD
practitioners.firm: The Law Offices of Jeffery M. Leving, Ltd.
named_cases: Co-author, Illinois Joint Custody Law
Co-author, Illinois Virtual Visitation statute
Author, Fathers' Rights: Hard-Hitting and Fair Advice for Every Father Involved in a Custody Dispute
Author, Divorce Wars: A Field Guide to the Winning Tactics
practitioners.hook: Legislative co-author — the policy/statute angle distinguishes him from pure litigators.
practitioners.notes: Specific named appellate citations not publicly confirmed on firm site.

practitioners.name: Charles D. Jamieson, JD
practitioners.firm: The Law Firm of Charles D. Jamieson, P.A.
named_cases: Lead counsel or consultant in 20+ states on PA matters (per firm bio)
Florida Bar Board Certified Marital & Family Law specialist
practitioners.hook: Florida Bar Board Certification — high-credential signal for any directory that vets credentials.
practitioners.notes: Named appellate cases not publicly confirmed on firm site.

practitioners.name: Joan S. Meier, JD
practitioners.firm: George Washington University Law School — National Family Violence Law Center
named_cases: Lead author, NIJ-funded U.S. Child Custody Outcomes empirical study (2019)
Co-drafter, Keeping Children Safe from Family Violence Act (Kayden's Law) — VAWA 2022 reauthorization
At DV LEAP: co-author of 11 amicus and 3 party briefs in US courts
practitioners.hook: Most-cited critique-camp scholar in US PA litigation; her NIJ data backs Kayden's Law.
practitioners.notes: State bar enumeration beyond DC not publicly confirmed; clerked US 7th Circuit. Office phone (202) 994-2278 per CV at https://www.law.gwu.edu/sites/g/files/zaxdzs5421/files/2026-02/joan-meier-cv-spring-2025.pdf

practitioners.name: Toby G. Kleinman, JD
practitioners.firm: Adler & Kleinman; adjunct, Nova Southeastern University
named_cases: Domestic Abuse, Child Custody, and Visitation: Winning in Family Court (Oxford UP, 2017, with Daniel Pollack)
Social Work and the Courts: A Casebook (with Daniel Pollack)
Chapters in The Broken Family Court anthology
Consultant in DV/child-abuse cases across 45+ states
practitioners.hook: NJ contact for PA-misuse cases; Harvard School of Public Health teaching bridges to public-health framing.
practitioners.notes: Firm 732-878-1170 (988 River Road, Piscataway, NJ 08854); direct line 732-309-5952.

practitioners.name: Paul Griffin, JD
named_cases: 20+ years litigating DV and child-abuse custody cases
Trains attorneys on appellate strategy in PA-based custody rulings
Quoted in ProPublica's PA investigation (interview source)
practitioners.hook: Child Justice is one of the only US legal-services orgs explicitly built to appeal PA-based custody decisions.
practitioners.notes: Phone 301-283-1762; office 8720 Georgia Avenue, Suite 703, Silver Spring, MD 20910. Specific named appellate citations not publicly confirmed on Child Justice site. JD Georgetown 1997.

practitioners.name: Margaret Drew, JD
practitioners.firm: University of Massachusetts School of Law — Associate Professor of Law
named_cases: Co-editor (with Jean Mercer), Challenging Parental Alienation: New Directions for Professionals and Parents (Routledge, 2022)
Prior trial and appellate practice representing DV survivors
practitioners.hook: Edited the academic-press counter-volume to the Lorandos/Bernet treatise — the direct intellectual counterweight pairing.
practitioners.notes: Specific state bar enumeration and direct email not publicly confirmed at provided URL; verify via UMass Law directory.

practitioners.name: Stephanie A. Tabashneck, PsyD, JD
practitioners.firm: Solo forensic practice (Wellesley/Boston, MA); Senior Fellow CLBB-MGH + Petrie-Flom Center, Harvard Law School
named_cases: Editor, Substance Use and Parenting: Best Practices for Family Court Practitioners (ABA)
Co-first author, White Paper on the Science of Late Adolescence: A Guide for Judges, Attorneys, and Policy Makers
President, Massachusetts Chapter, AFCC
practitioners.hook: Rare dual-credentialed (PsyD + JD) practitioner — useful for bridging recognition/critique in MA.
practitioners.notes: Original task brief described her as DV+PA publisher; her own bio frames her work as substance use + complex custody (incl. contact-refusal coaching), so stance positioned as middle, not critique.

practitioners.name: William A. (Bill) Eddy, LCSW, JD/Esq., CFLS
practitioners.firm: High Conflict Institute (co-founder, CINO); Pepperdine Caruso School of Law Straus Institute faculty
named_cases: 15 years as Certified Family Law Specialist, State Bar of California
Author of multiple books on high-conflict personalities in family court
Conjoint associate professor, University of Newcastle Law School (Australia)
practitioners.hook: His 'high-conflict personality' frame is the most-used neutral vocabulary in US family courts when neither pure-PA nor pure-DV framing fits.
practitioners.notes: Primarily a trainer/author now; current active bar status not publicly confirmed.

practitioners.name: Hon. Donna J. Martinson (retired)
practitioners.firm: Retired BC Supreme Court Justice; Adjunct, SFU School of Criminology (FREDA Centre) and Allard School of Law, UBC
named_cases: A.A. v S.N.A. 2007 BCSC 594 (PA sole-custody ruling)
Co-author, Family Violence and Evolving Judicial Roles: Judges as Equality Guardians in Family Law Cases, (2017) 30:1 Can J Fam L 11
practitioners.hook: Her trajectory (recognition-camp ruling, then DV-aware writing) is rare evidence that the two camps can be bridged judicially.
practitioners.notes: NOT US-admitted. Included as cross-jurisdictional reference only; move to a Canadian list. country: CA.

excluded_with_reason.name: Steve Miller, MD
excluded_with_reason.reason: Deceased 2022; was an MD (Harvard Medical School faculty), not a JD.

excluded_with_reason.name: Karen Anderson Hyde
excluded_with_reason.reason: Closest match is Karen Anna Anderson (CA Bar #167056), a Long Beach family lawyer — no demonstrable PA-specific publication, appellate, or expert-witness record found.

excluded_with_reason.name: Adrienne Adams, MD
excluded_with_reason.reason: No verifiable US-based 'Adrienne Adams, MD' with PA publications. Closest match is UK academic Dr Adrienne Barnett — flag for UK list.

excluded_with_reason.name: Hannah Brenner Johnson, JD
excluded_with_reason.reason: California Western law professor focused on sexual violence in institutional settings, not PA/custody specifically.

excluded_with_reason.name: Hon. Anne McGowan
excluded_with_reason.reason: Could not be uniquely identified from public sources.

excluded_with_reason.name: Charlotte Proudman
excluded_with_reason.reason: UK barrister (Right to Equality); not US-admitted. Flag for UK list.

excluded_with_reason.name: Robert A. Evans, PhD
excluded_with_reason.reason: PhD school psychologist, not JD. Belongs on experts/expert-witness list, not lawyers.

excluded_with_reason.name: Joseph Goldberg
excluded_with_reason.reason: Consultant; JD admission status not publicly confirmed. Belongs on experts/consultants list. Public sources locate him in FL/Toronto, not Houston TX as task brief suggested.

methodology_notes.verification_principle: Primary sources only (firm sites, bar profiles, published works, named press coverage). Stance characterization must be sourced — never inferred. Unconfirmed facts flagged 'not publicly confirmed'.
honest_gaps: Joan Meier's state bar admissions beyond DC not publicly confirmed

Specific named appellate citations for Joshi, Leving, Jamieson, Lorandos, and Griffin not enumerated on firm sites (their PA work is documented via books, articles, statutes, and press coverage instead)
Margaret Drew's direct email and bar admission state not publicly confirmed at faculty page
Bill Eddy's current active bar status not publicly confirmed (long-time CFLS but now primarily trainer/author)
Joseph Goldberg's JD status not confirmed; recommend moving him to a separate experts/consultants directory
Robert Evans is a PhD, not JD; same recommendation
Original brief named several candidates (Karen Anderson Hyde, Adrienne Adams MD, Hon. Anne McGowan, Steve Miller as JD) that could not be verified as US-admitted JDs
AFCC-affiliated lawyers as a group: Tabashneck and Pruett both surfaced, but Pruett is PhD not JD; Sullivan is PhD; only Tabashneck qualifies as a JD-credentialed AFCC affiliate from the search batch
